source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Alfonso the Ostrich has been training for the Head in the Sand Competition in the Animolympiad. He buried his head in the sand last week and pulled it out at 8.15 am on Monday to find he had reached a new personal record - he had been underground for 98 hours and 56 minutes. When did Alfonso bury his head in the sand? <image1>

image_dependency: question_text is fully self-contained as a time-arithmetic word problem
an image file exists in asset_registry_record, but no visual reference is needed by the wording
multi_step: solving requires subtracting 98 hours and 56 minutes from 8:15 am Monday
this involves at least day-level rollover plus minute/hour subtraction
multiple-choice options indicate a single computed result rather than direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a specific timestamp/day expression
the result can be checked deterministically by backward time calculation
multiple-choice candidates provide an additional consistency check

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present with no registry issues
the problem is text-self-contained, so the low image dependency does not reduce recoverability
high verifiability score indicates the answer can be checked deterministically
multi-step reasoning requirement gives the sample useful problem-solving value
the only risk flag is that the image appears nonessential, which is not a core semantic deficiency
